title: A Quiet Place: Day One Audience Score Analysis
text: A Quiet Place: Day One achieved the lowest audience score in the franchise, landing at 73% on Rotten Tomatoes, compared to the original film's 83% and Part II's 92%. Viewers complained about several aspects of the prequel. One audience member noted: Just not many suspenseful scenes. Wished they would have followed the same family as the other movies. Others found the pacing problematic, with some reporting they fell asleep during screenings. A recurring complaint involved the lack of explanation regarding the alien invaders. As one reviewer pointed out, despite three films in the franchise, audiences still know virtually nothing about the creatures' origins or motivations. While the film underperformed critically and with audiences, it broke box office records, earning $53 million during its opening three days surpassing both previous installments in the franchise. Professional critics agreed with audiences more often than typical for horror films. The film's 84% critics score still qualifies as fresh, but falls significantly short of the original (96%) and Part II (90%). Lupita Nyong'o's lead performance and the film's cat character, Frodo, received consistent praise from reviewers.
